title: Garlicky Australian Duck Pasta
description: A simple, one-pot dish featuring duck fat, pasta-water-emulsion, and a shojin-friendly twist. Perfect for a weeknight dinner or a special occasion.
instructions: Heat the duck fat in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 2 minutes, until fragrant.
Add the chopped lemongrass and cook for 1 minute, until slightly softened.
Add the pappardelle pasta, chicken broth, and white wine, and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 10-12 minutes, until the pasta is al dente.
Stir in the toasted pumpkin seeds and cook for 1 minute, until combined.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Optional: garnish with chopped parsley before serving.
Let the pasta rest, covered, for 2-3 minutes before serving.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
